steps.input:
python3 - <<'PY'
import zipfile, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]')
with zipfile.ZipFile(p) as z:
    for n in z.namelist():
        if n.endswith('.xml'):
            ET.fromstring(z.read(n))
    text='\n'.join(z.read(n).decode('utf-8') for n in z.namelist() if n.endswith('.xml'))
    for pat in ['Calc!D10','Calc!D3','Sensitivity!E18','Sensitivity!F18','Sensitivity!H18','R1','R2','R3']:
        print(pat, text.count(pat))
PY

input.cmd:
python3 - <<'PY'
import zipfile, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]')
with zipfile.ZipFile(p) as z:
    for n in z.namelist():
        if n.endswith('.xml'):
            ET.fromstring(z.read(n))
    text='\n'.join(z.read(n).decode('utf-8') for n in z.namelist() if n.endswith('.xml'))
    for pat in ['Calc!D10','Calc!D3','Sensitivity!E18','Sensitivity!F18','Sensitivity!H18','R1','R2','R3']:
        print(pat, text.count(pat))
PY

task: Advanced high-value task: Thermal Process Lethality Point Robustness Evidence Review. Scenario: a specialist reviewer found conflicting signals and needs an auditable explanation of which values matter for thermal process lethality point. Embedded packet: Records: R1 temperature_C=71 duration_min=107 z_value_C=6; R2 temperature_C=80 duration_min=103 z_value_C=9; R3 temperature_C=66 duration_min=118 z_value_C=5. Remediation options: O1 changes temperature_C by -12% cost=92000; O2 changes duration_min by -18% cost=106000; O3 changes z_value_C by -25% cost=64000; max_budget=132000. Rules and constraints: lethality=duration*10^((T-80)/z); cumulative target>=12 equivalent minutes; missing values cannot be imputed and all calculations must use only the embedded packet. Required work: design the output schema and acceptance checks an automated agent would need to reproduce the analysis. Deliver thermal_lethality_2.xlsx + expert_memo.md with formulas, intermediate values, row-level citations, explicit assumptions, sensitivity checks, and a final recommendation table. Do not invent inputs; mark ambiguous items as needs_review.
